column2: Hard catalog-DRO fixed-final-time mixed-regularized tail-coast locked nominal provenance with the final five nominal control segments fixed exactly to zero; no branch optimizer is run.
column4: ballistic propagation of target_dro_seed for transfer_time
column57: optimizer_success is seed nominal optimizer success AND tail-coast nominal optimizer success AND branch_optimizer_all_success. branch_optimizer_all_success requires at least one selected branch and optimizer_success=True for every selected branch's accepted portfolio-selected result; threshold-feasible no-recovery-variable branches support backend/meets_thresholds but do not count as optimizer convergence
column69: branch_portfolio_all_success is retained for CSV compatibility and means every selected branch's accepted portfolio-selected result has optimizer_success=True; it does not mean every evaluated portfolio candidate, weight variant, or fallback initialization converged
column70: no selected branch portfolio candidates were evaluated
column129: Fixed-final-time tail-coast locked-nominal continuous baseline: a nominal all-windows trajectory is solved first, then refined with the final tail_coast_segments nominal controls fixed exactly to zero. Each selected branch keeps the original target and original transfer time. If branch initialization fallbacks are configured, the nominal post-outage initialization is evaluated first across the branch weight portfolio and fallbacks are evaluated only when that nominal-start portfolio has no optimizer-converged threshold-feasible candidate. The row-level portfolio_acceptance_rule summarizes whether any selected branch evaluated or accepted fallback initializations; per-branch branch_results keep the branch-specific acceptance rules.
column130: selected_outages=0; no fixed-final-time branch optimizer is run
column131: For selected masks, controls before outage_end(mask) are locked tail-coast nominal controls with missed segment(s) zeroed. Controls from outage_end(mask) through the original final time are optimization variables.
column132: all_outage_errors evaluates every configured outage mask at the original fixed final time. Selected masks use optimized branches; unselected masks use masked locked tail-coast nominal controls.
column133: Every nominal and recovery acceleration vector is projected to the Euclidean ball ||u_i|| <= amax before propagation, residual evaluation, fuel computation, and reporting. The nominal tail controls are then reported as exact zeros.
column134: Branch optimization never changes nominal_controls. nominal_tail_coast_error is measured at the original transfer time against the original target after enforcing the exact-zero nominal tail coast.
column135: No delayed target state or added recovery horizon is used; branch_total_duration equals cfg.tf for every row.
column136: selected_worst_error is the worst original-target terminal error at the fixed final time among selected independently optimized tail-coast locked-nominal branches
column137: fixed-final-time tail-coast locked nominal branch recovery complete

column2: Hard catalog-DRO fixed-final-time recovery for all one-segment missed-thrust masks using a mixed-regularized five-segment exact-zero nominal tail coast, regularized/terminal-only branch residual portfolio variants, and deterministic constant-vector branch-start fallbacks.
column4: ballistic propagation of target_dro_seed for transfer_time

column69: branch_portfolio_all_success is retained for CSV compatibility and means every selected branch's accepted portfolio-selected result has optimizer_success=True; it does not mean every evaluated portfolio candidate, weight variant, or fallback initialization converged
column70: row summary across 14 selected branches: fallback initializations were evaluated by 1 branch(es), and a fallback initialization was accepted by 1 branch(es); per-branch portfolio_acceptance_rule entries in branch_results remain authoritative

column129: Fixed-final-time tail-coast locked-nominal continuous baseline: a nominal all-windows trajectory is solved first, then refined with the final tail_coast_segments nominal controls fixed exactly to zero. Each selected branch keeps the original target and original transfer time. If branch initialization fallbacks are configured, the nominal post-outage initialization is evaluated first across the branch weight portfolio and fallbacks are evaluated only when that nominal-start portfolio has no optimizer-converged threshold-feasible candidate. The row-level portfolio_acceptance_rule summarizes whether any selected branch evaluated or accepted fallback initializations; per-branch branch_results keep the branch-specific acceptance rules.
column130: all one-segment outage masks are selected for fixed-final-time tail-coast locked-nominal recovery
column131: For selected masks, controls before outage_end(mask) are locked tail-coast nominal controls with missed segment(s) zeroed. Controls from outage_end(mask) through the original final time are optimization variables.
column132: all_outage_errors evaluates every configured outage mask at the original fixed final time. Selected masks use optimized branches; unselected masks use masked locked tail-coast nominal controls.
column133: Every nominal and recovery acceleration vector is projected to the Euclidean ball ||u_i|| <= amax before propagation, residual evaluation, fuel computation, and reporting. The nominal tail controls are then reported as exact zeros.
column134: Branch optimization never changes nominal_controls. nominal_tail_coast_error is measured at the original transfer time against the original target after enforcing the exact-zero nominal tail coast.
column135: No delayed target state or added recovery horizon is used; branch_total_duration equals cfg.tf for every row.
column136: selected_worst_error is the worst original-target terminal error at the fixed final time among selected independently optimized tail-coast locked-nominal branches
column137: fixed-final-time tail-coast locked nominal branch recovery complete

column2: Hard catalog-DRO fixed-final-time recovery for all two-segment missed-thrust masks using a mixed-regularized five-segment exact-zero nominal tail coast, regularized/terminal-only branch residual portfolio variants, and deterministic constant-vector branch-start fallbacks.
column4: ballistic propagation of target_dro_seed for transfer_time
column57: optimizer_success is seed nominal optimizer success AND tail-coast nominal optimizer success AND branch_optimizer_all_success. branch_optimizer_all_success requires at least one selected branch and optimizer_success=True for every selected branch's accepted portfolio-selected result; threshold-feasible no-recovery-variable branches support backend/meets_thresholds but do not count as optimizer convergence
column69: branch_portfolio_all_success is retained for CSV compatibility and means every selected branch's accepted portfolio-selected result has optimizer_success=True; it does not mean every evaluated portfolio candidate, weight variant, or fallback initialization converged
column70: row summary across 13 selected branches: fallback initializations were evaluated by 3 branch(es), and a fallback initialization was accepted by 3 branch(es); per-branch portfolio_acceptance_rule entries in branch_results remain authoritative

column129: Fixed-final-time tail-coast locked-nominal continuous baseline: a nominal all-windows trajectory is solved first, then refined with the final tail_coast_segments nominal controls fixed exactly to zero. Each selected branch keeps the original target and original transfer time. If branch initialization fallbacks are configured, the nominal post-outage initialization is evaluated first across the branch weight portfolio and fallbacks are evaluated only when that nominal-start portfolio has no optimizer-converged threshold-feasible candidate. The row-level portfolio_acceptance_rule summarizes whether any selected branch evaluated or accepted fallback initializations; per-branch branch_results keep the branch-specific acceptance rules.
column130: all configured outage masks are selected for fixed-final-time tail-coast locked-nominal recovery
column131: For selected masks, controls before outage_end(mask) are locked tail-coast nominal controls with missed segment(s) zeroed. Controls from outage_end(mask) through the original final time are optimization variables.
column132: all_outage_errors evaluates every configured outage mask at the original fixed final time. Selected masks use optimized branches; unselected masks use masked locked tail-coast nominal controls.
column133: Every nominal and recovery acceleration vector is projected to the Euclidean ball ||u_i|| <= amax before propagation, residual evaluation, fuel computation, and reporting. The nominal tail controls are then reported as exact zeros.
column134: Branch optimization never changes nominal_controls. nominal_tail_coast_error is measured at the original transfer time against the original target after enforcing the exact-zero nominal tail coast.
column135: No delayed target state or added recovery horizon is used; branch_total_duration equals cfg.tf for every row.
column136: selected_worst_error is the worst original-target terminal error at the fixed final time among selected independently optimized tail-coast locked-nominal branches
column137: fixed-final-time tail-coast locked nominal branch recovery complete
